description: Mandarin Tone 3 (low-dipping, 213) undergoes a systematic allophonic change when followed by another Tone 3 syllable: it becomes a rising tone (35) similar to Tone 2. This tone sandhi process is one of the most well-documented phonological rules in Mandarin and applies obligatorily in connected speech.
sources: Chao, Y. R. (1968). _A Grammar of Spoken Chinese_. University of California Press.
Chen, M. (2003). _Tone Sandhi: Patterns Across Chinese Dialects_. Cambridge University Press.
